_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The Climate Change Authority Act 2011 (C2022C00255) is Australian Commonwealth legislation that establishes an independent statutory authority — the Climate Change Authority (CCA) — and tasks it with providing expert climate policy advice to the Australian Government. The Act does not directly impose obligations on private commercial operators such as freight forwarders. It is a framework governance statute: it creates the institutional actor (the CCA) whose reviews and advice shape the downstream regulatory instruments that do bind industry — principally the National Greenhouse and Energy Reporting Act 2007 (NGER), the Carbon Credits (Carbon Farming Initiative) Act 2011 (CFI Act/ACCU Scheme), and, following the 2022 amendments, the Climate Change Act 2022.

For a freight forwarder operating across AU-origin and AU-destination trade lanes (Americas, Europe, Asia), the workspace sits in the compliance chain as an **indirect subject**: the CCA's periodic reviews and annual progress advice inform regulatory tightening of the Safeguard Mechanism, ACCU prices, and NGER reporting thresholds. Large-emitter shipper clients ("designated large facilities" under NGER) who rely on the workspace for freight forwarding have direct compliance obligations; the workspace is a service provider in their emissions measurement and abatement chain rather than a directly regulated entity under this Act.

The workspace's role placement requiring legal confirmation: whether any consolidated freight volume arrangement the workspace operates in Australia meets the threshold for NGER-designated-facility status (currently 25,000 tCO₂-e per year scope 1+2). If it does, NGER and Safeguard Mechanism obligations — not the CCA Act itself — would apply directly. The CCA Act is the institutional parent; the operative compliance instruments are its daughter statutes. Monitoring CCA outputs is therefore a leading-indicator activity for the workspace, not a direct compliance obligation.

*Source: Climate Change Authority Act 2011 (C2022C00255), Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water, 14 September 2022. https://www.legislation.gov.au/Details/C2022C00255; Climate Change Authority Act 2011 — Climate Change Laws of the World, Grantham Research Institute, n.d. https://climate-laws.org/document/climate-change-authority-act-2011_c4f5*
